title: The Hard Ethics of Editing Embryos
script_summary: Exploring the ethical dilemmas surrounding germline editing in human embryos.

segments.voiceover: Editing human DNA could change life as we know it—but should it?
The world watched in shock after He Jiankui edited embryos, sparking a fierce debate on ethics.
Most scientists now agree: the risks of germline editing are immense and unpredictable.
Potential genetic mutations or unforeseen health issues could arise, affecting generations.
Ethical lines are being drawn; many fear crossing them could lead to a dystopian future.
What do you think? Should we edit the human genome? Comment below!

full_voiceover: Editing human DNA could change life as we know it—but should it? The world watched in shock after He Jiankui edited embryos, sparking a fierce debate on ethics. Most scientists now agree: the risks of germline editing are immense and unpredictable. Potential genetic mutations or unforeseen health issues could arise, affecting generations. Ethical lines are being drawn; many fear crossing them could lead to a dystopian future. What do you think? Should we edit the human genome? Comment below!
call_to_action: What do you think? Should we edit the human genome? Comment below!
